Trading TTC · pattern detectors vs your eye

Disagreement Gallery — where the code reads the week the other way

15 weeks where the detectors and your hand-labels disagree on direction. Look at each chart, tell me what your eye saw, and your answer becomes the rule.

Disagreement Gallery · direction rule

These are the weeks where the mechanical detectors and your own hand-labels disagree on the single most important read: which way the week actually reversed — was the tradable trap a top to sell, or a bottom to buy? Each card shows the chart the code looked at, your label and the code's read side by side, and one plain question for you to answer. Your answers become the direction rule the code is missing.

The honest problem Across all 36 labelled weeks the code gets direction right only about half the time (~50%) — a coin flip. Regime, retests, volume and levels it can measure; but which side is the real fake-out is a discretionary read the weekly geometry alone does not recover. That read lives in your eye, not in the maths. Everything downstream — peak day, session, the trade side — cascades from it, so this is the one input worth getting from you.

Of the 36 weeks, 26 carry a load-bearing disagreement (regime, trigger-type, or trigger-direction). This gallery shows the 15 where direction itself flips — the most instructive set. 11 further load-bearing weeks are left out on purpose (they disagree only on regime or trigger-type; direction agreed), and another 9 weeks differ only on softer columns (retest / BBWP / levels / session / peak-day). Cards are numbered and dated.

How to answer Just reply in chat, one week at a time, by its date — e.g. “2025-08-04: the real trap was the Monday high getting sold; my peak was the Sunday-night spike, not Tuesday.” Say what your eye saw: which extreme was the fake-out, which candle you called the peak, and why it was a top or a bottom. Rough and in your own words is perfect.

? Your question for week 1 (2025-08-04): You read a Final-Damage M — a top that got sold, peaking Monday, a small ~2.7% fade. The code read the mirror image: a W bottom on Tuesday that it bought for ~5.8% up, and it called the whole week a clean Trend rather than a fake-move week. Which was the trap you'd actually have traded — the Monday high failing, or the Tuesday low holding? And what told your eye “still FMWB” when the code saw a trend? Why this matters: the code and your eye disagree on direction here — the one read the machine can't recover from geometry alone. Your answer becomes the rule that picks the side.
